articles.title: Ebola outbreak in DRC is 'spreading rapidly' with almost 750 suspected cases: WHO chief
articles.summary: WHO Director-General Tedros warned the DRC Ebola outbreak is spreading rapidly, citing 82 confirmed and nearly 750 suspected cases as of May 22, with national risk upgraded to VERY HIGH.

articles.title: First meeting of the IHR Emergency Committee — Temporary recommendations
articles.summary: WHO IHR Emergency Committee issued the first temporary recommendations for the PHEIC, directing DRC and Uganda to declare health emergencies and strengthen contact tracing; no approved vaccines or therapeutics exist for Bundibugyo virus.

articles.title: India-Africa summit postponed as Ebola spreads to M23-held DR Congo area
articles.summary: First confirmed Ebola case announced in South Kivu's M23-controlled territory; AU and India jointly postponed the India-Africa Forum Summit in New Delhi.

articles.title: American doctor with Ebola flown to Germany as his wife and children are monitored
articles.summary: Dr. Peter Stafford, a US medical missionary who tested positive for Bundibugyo ebolavirus after unknowingly operating on an infected patient, was evacuated to Berlin's Charite University Hospital; family monitored in DRC.

articles.title: Ebola virus disease outbreak — DRC and Uganda, 19 May 2026
articles.summary: ECDC update: 64 confirmed cases in DRC (60 Ituri, 4 North Kivu including Goma + newly affected Butembo) plus 2 in Uganda; over 650 suspected cases with 160 suspected deaths.

articles.title: US bans entry from Ebola-affected countries as American patient is identified
articles.summary: The administration invoked Title 42 to bar non-US nationals who had been in DRC, Uganda, or South Sudan within the prior 21 days — the first US Ebola-specific travel ban.

articles.title: Rwanda closes Rubavu-Goma border over Ebola fears
articles.summary: Rwanda unilaterally closes the Rubavu-Goma border crossing indefinitely after a confirmed Ebola case in Goma. Only DRC/Rwandan nationals returning home permitted. WHO has advised against border closures and travel restrictions.

articles.title: New Ebola outbreak fuels mounting global alarm as US works to relocate affected Americans
articles.summary: Suspected cases rise to 395+ with 100+ deaths. Field hospitals being established in Bunia as existing facilities are overwhelmed. 6 Americans exposed, 1 symptomatic, evacuation to German military base under discussion.

articles.title: CDC implements enhanced screening at US ports of entry for Ebola
articles.summary: CDC and DHS implement enhanced travel screening, entry restrictions, and public health measures at US ports of entry for travelers from DRC. DRC at Travel Level 2 (Enhanced Precautions), Uganda at Level 1.

articles.title: Congo-Uganda Ebola outbreak: WHO declares global health emergency — what to know
articles.summary: Comprehensive overview of the escalating outbreak. Goma case threatens a city of 1M+ people under rebel control. No vaccine exists for Bundibugyo virus. CEPI/Moderna/Oxford preclinical vaccine efforts funded but years from availability.

articles.title: WHO declares Ebola outbreak in DRC and Uganda a PHEIC
articles.summary: WHO Director-General declares the Bundibugyo ebolavirus epidemic a Public Health Emergency of International Concern. Key factors: no approved vaccine or therapeutics for this species, confirmed cross-border transmission to Uganda, rising case numbers with significant surveillance uncertainty, and ongoing armed conflict in Ituri Province.

articles.title: Ebola virus disease — DRC and Uganda — Disease Outbreak News DON602
articles.summary: WHO publishes DON602 with updated case counts: 12 confirmed cases (10 DRC, 2 Uganda), 336 suspected cases, 89 total deaths. Confirmed case reported in Goma, North Kivu. WHO warns outbreak may be larger than currently detected.

articles.title: CDC mobilizes international Ebola response
articles.summary: CDC announces 30+ staff already in DRC country office with additional deployments planned. Providing lab diagnostics support and coordinating potential evacuation of exposed Americans. Emphasizes no licensed vaccine exists for Bundibugyo ebolavirus.

articles.title: WHO declares Ebola outbreak in DRC a public health emergency
articles.summary: ECDC monitoring statement following WHO PHEIC declaration. Risk to EU/EEA residents rated very low. Discussing potential EU Health Task Force deployment.

articles.title: Ebola outbreak declared global emergency: What you need to know
articles.summary: Comprehensive explainer on the Ituri Province Ebola epidemic. Key points: Bundibugyo virus lacks any approved vaccine or treatment, 8 of 13 initial samples tested positive (62%), at least 4 healthcare workers have died, and the outbreak is occurring in an active conflict zone with Islamic State-affiliated militants.

articles.title: WHO declares Ebola outbreak in DRC, Uganda a global emergency — what to know
articles.summary: Coverage of WHO PHEIC declaration with context on the Bundibugyo virus species, the significance of cross-border spread to Uganda, and challenges posed by armed conflict and limited health infrastructure in Ituri Province.

articles.title: Americans exposed to Ebola in Congo as outbreak grows
articles.summary: Several American nationals in DRC believed to have had contact with suspected Ebola cases. At least one may have developed symptoms. US government arranging potential evacuation, possibly to a military base in Germany.

articles.title: Africa CDC calls for urgent regional coordination following Ebola outbreak in DRC and imported case in Uganda
articles.summary: Africa CDC convenes high-level consultative meeting with 130+ participants from WHO, UN agencies, donor countries, and pharmaceutical companies. Director-General to visit affected countries. Incident Management Support Team activated.

articles.title: New Ebola outbreak in DRC declared a global emergency by WHO
articles.summary: NPR reports on the outbreak dynamics, noting the diagnostic delay caused by standard rapid tests only detecting Zaire ebolavirus. Bundibugyo-specific assays were required, adding approximately 10 days to confirmation.

articles.title: DRC Health Minister warns of very high Ebola lethality rate
articles.summary: DRC Health Minister issues warning about the high case fatality rate among suspected cases. Total suspected deaths reach 80. Healthcare worker infections highlight nosocomial transmission risk.

articles.title: Africa CDC issues continental alert for Ebola in Ituri Province
articles.summary: Africa CDC activates continental alert following DRC official outbreak declaration. South Sudan flagged as highest-risk neighboring country due to shared border with Ituri Province.

articles.title: What to know about the Ebola outbreak that WHO declared a global health emergency
articles.summary: PBS overview of the outbreak, noting this is DRC's 17th Ebola epidemic but the first caused by Bundibugyo virus in the country. All previous DRC outbreaks were Zaire ebolavirus.

articles.title: INRB Kinshasa confirms Bundibugyo ebolavirus in Ituri samples
articles.summary: National reference laboratory INRB in Kinshasa confirms Bundibugyo ebolavirus in 8 of 13 blood samples from Mongbwalu Health Zone. Standard Ebola Zaire rapid diagnostic tests had previously returned negative, causing a 10-day delay in identification.

articles.title: WHO alerted to high-mortality illness in Mongbwalu Health Zone
articles.summary: WHO receives alert about clusters of unexplained deaths in Mongbwalu, including healthcare workers. Rapid response team dispatched to investigate. Initial Ebola Zaire rapid tests return negative.
